description: Posts that directly ask followers opinion-based or preference questions about food/beverages, encouraging comment responses and community participation.
structural_recipe: Lead with a product or recipe presentation, follow with a casual direct question (e.g., 'do you like X?', 'which café makes it best?', 'is this easy or complicated?'). Use colloquial Arabic, add emoji, include brand or relevant hashtags. Keep tone friendly and conversational.
why_it_works: Saudis value opinion-sharing and community feedback in food culture. Direct questions bypass passive scrolling and create a sense of personal address. The casual dialect and emojis lower barriers to responding, while preference questions tap into regional coffee/beverage expertise pride.
transplantation_caveats: Requires authentic colloquial Arabic—formal MSA reduces engagement
Questions must relate to accessible products/experiences in Saudi market
Avoid yes/no questions; preference-based questions perform better
Response time matters—early engagement signals boost algorithmic reach
cultural_constraints: Must respect local food preferences and Islamic dietary sensitivities
Colloquial tone essential to feel authentic in Saudi social media context
